tables.title: The H's and T's — Reversible Causes of Cardiac Arrest
rows: Flat IVC on US, hemorrhage history, dehydration
Ensure advanced airway, 100% O₂, confirm ETCO₂
Low pH on blood gas, prolonged arrest
Improve ventilation; treat the cause; avoid empiric bicarb
ECG changes (peaked T / wide QRS for hyperkalemia)
Ca²⁺, bicarb, insulin/glucose, albuterol for hyperkalemia
Active rewarming; do not stop until ≥32°C
Absent breath sounds, tracheal deviation, JVD
Muffled heart sounds, JVD, pulsus paradoxus, US effusion
Specific antidotes (Fab for digoxin, intralipid, etc.)
DVT risk factors, RV strain on US, PE history
Acute MI on ECG, ischemic changes

tables.title: Antiarrhythmic Drugs for Refractory VF/pVT
rows: Specifically for polymorphic VT (torsades de pointes)
Give as soon as feasible for all arrest rhythms

tables.title: Defibrillation Strategies for Refractory VF/pVT
rows: 200 J via standard anterolateral or anterior-posterior pads
Reposition pads to alter current path through myocardium
After ≥3 failed shocks with standard placement
Two defibrillators, asynchronous rapid succession, different pad positions
Selective; after ≥3 failed shocks when available
